name: Build a CLI tool from scratch
task: Create a Python CLI tool at /tmp/eval_wc.py that counts lines, words, and characters in a file (like wc). Invoked as `python3 /tmp/eval_wc.py <filename>`, it must print a single line exactly in the form '{lines} {words} {chars} {filename}' (four space-separated fields): the number of newline-terminated lines, whitespace-separated words, and characters (bytes) in the file, then the filename argument verbatim. If the file does not exist, print a clear error message (to stderr or stdout) and exit with status code 1.

validation.script: set -e; printf 'hello world\nfoo bar baz\n' > /tmp/eval_wc_test.txt; OUT=$(python3 /tmp/eval_wc.py /tmp/eval_wc_test.txt) || { echo 'tool failed on valid file'; exit 1; }; set -- $OUT; [ "$1" = '2' ] || { echo "lines: got $1 want 2 (full: $OUT)"; exit 1; }; [ "$2" = '5' ] || { echo "words: got $2 want 5 (full: $OUT)"; exit 1; }; [ "$3" = '24' ] || { echo "chars: got $3 want 24 (full: $OUT)"; exit 1; }; case "$OUT" in *eval_wc_test.txt*) : ;; *) echo 'filename missing from output'; exit 1;; esac; RC=0; python3 /tmp/eval_wc.py /nonexistent_xyz >/dev/null 2>&1 || RC=$?; [ "$RC" -eq 1 ] || { echo "missing-file exit code got $RC want 1"; exit 1; }; rm -f /tmp/eval_wc_test.txt; echo OK
